titleStmt.title: SII 42.298: original edition by C Somasundara Rao
No. 298. (A.R. No. 66 of 1956-57.) Donakoṇḍa, Darsi Taluk, Nellore District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a slab set up in the temple of Gangamma.

Dated Śaka 1315 (1328), Vyaya, Kārttika, ba. 5, Sunday, corresponding to A.D. 1406, October 31. [Authors of N.D.I. read 1328, while the figures look like 1315. However as Vyaya corresponds to Śaka 1328, and as the astonomical details agree for Śaka 1328, that date may be accepted].

Records that somebody undertook the construction of the temple for Māṁkanama (Mahaṁ kāḷamma ?), shrine for heroes (Vīrula-guḍi) and compound-wall, expending 195 ḍha(ṭa)ṁkas of money when Kaṁpadēvani Chāma-voḍeya was ruling over the village. (See N.D.I. D. 19, pp. 301 ff).

[This record, as well as No. 299- A.R. No. 67 of 1956-57 are on a white marble pillar with a half lotus design on one of its side faces, and fluted into an octogan above the design, similar to the āyaka pillars of Nāgārjunakoṇḍa type].

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī śaka
varuṣaṁbulu
1328 gu nēṁ
ṭi vyaya saṁva
tsara kārttika ba 5
Ā Ēṁbhaiyāru
dēśālunu sthira śrī
ḥlai subhikṣamugā
nunna donakoṁḍa
grāmamu vūṁ
rjitamugānunnu
dēvarāya ma
mahārāyuṁ

ḍu ra
jyama(mē)lu
cuṁḍaṁ
(ḍa)gānu
kaṁpadē
vani
cāma
voḍa
yalu
[I]grāma
mēlu
cuṁ
ḍaṁgānu

donakoṁḍa ti
rumaṁḍula pa
tinu Ōjeka kō
maḷaku puṇya
mugānu ta
nakuṁgala dhanamu
195 ḍha(ṭaṁ)kālu
vecciṁci māṁ
kanamaku kalugu
ḍi gaṭṭiṁcci vīrula
guḍigaṭṭiṁci pra(prā)kā
ramu kōṭaveṭṭiṁ
cenu Ācaṁdrārkkasthā
I(yi)gānu cēsenu ma
ṁgaḷa mahā śrī
Digital edition of SII 42.298 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
